issue_title: Build the first episode setup flow
issue_body:
Create a runnable web app that opens directly into a creator-facing workflow for starting a podcast episode. The first screen should let a user name an episode, add a Riverside link or upload separate synced speaker video files, assign each source to speaker buckets like Host, Guest 1, and Guest 2, and add optional social links for each speaker. After setup, the user should land on a basic episode workspace summary that clearly shows the imported sources, speaker assignments, and captured context, ready for later style selection and editing.

## Acceptance (definition of done)
A reviewer can run the app locally from the repo and complete a new episode setup without editing code. They can enter an episode title, choose Riverside link or file upload mode, add at least three speaker sources, assign them to Host/Guest buckets, add social links per speaker, and continue to a workspace screen where all entered information is displayed accurately. The flow must handle missing required fields with clear creator-facing validation and include at least one automated test or documented runnable check covering the setup flow.

---
This is the current active step for this product. The maintainer will only merge a pull request that completes THIS step and demonstrably works. Open a PR that closes this issue.

_Why this step now: The repo has no product implementation yet, so the highest-value next move is a visible, runnable foundation around the creator's first required job: bringing raw synced recordings and speaker context into the product. This unlocks every later workflow, including preset styles, canvas editing, audio cleanup, captions, templates, and export, without spending the first step on internal plumbing or planning-only work._
